references.text: Agoo, EMG & DA Madulid. 2012. Cycas sancti-lasallei (Cycadaceae), a new species from the Philippines. Blumea 57: 131-133
Earle, CJ. 1997 onwards. The gymnosperm database
Laubenfels, DJ de & F Adema. 1998. A taxonomic revision of the genera Cycas and Epicycas gen. nov. (Cycadaceaea). Blumea 43: 351-400
Lindstrom AJ, K.D.Hill & LC Stanberg. 2008. The genus Cycas (Cycadaceae) in the Philippines. Telopea 12: 119-145
Madulid DA & EMG Agoo. 2005. A new species of Cycas (Cycadaceae) from the Philippines. Blumea 50: 519-522
Madulid, DA & EMG Agoo. 2009. Taxonomy and conservation of Philippine Cycads. Blumea 54: 99-102

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 21 March 2026

species.citation: K.D.Hill & A.Lindstr. , Telopea 12 (2008) 130; Type: E.S. Fernando 1617 (LBC, holo), Palawan: Puerto Princesa City, cult., 26-Jun-2001.
species.notes: Known only in cultivation in Puerto Princesa City, Palawan.

species.citation: (J.Schust.) K.D.Hill , Proc. Third Int. Confer. Cycad Biol. (1995) 150 ; Lindstrom et al., Telopea 12 (2008) 126; ≡ Cycas circinalis var. curranii J.Schust. , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (1) (1932) 69; Type: Curran 3842 (PNH, holo, lost; K, neo; P, isoneo), Palawan: Molinao River, on river bank, Mar-1906. ≠ Cycas circinalis auct. non L. ; Foxw., PJS 6 c (1911) Bot. 151, pro parte; Amoroso, PJS 115 (1986) 182. ≠ Cycas rumphii auct. non Miq. ; Merr., Sp. Blancoanae (1918) 52; EPFP 1 (1922) 1, pro parte. ≠ Cycas silvestris auct. non K.D.Hill ; de Laub. & Adema, Blumea 43 (1998) 372.
species.notes: Apparently confined to lowland ultramafics, occurring in the understorey of mixed closed forest, common on steep slopes, occasionally on alluvial outwash from ultramafic hills.

species.citation: de Laub. , Blumea 43 (1998) 373 ; Lindstrom et al., Telopea 12 (2008); Cortez et al., Check List 19 (2023) 987; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific Biodiv. 17 (2024) 10; Cañeda et al., Davao Research J. 16 (2025) 91; Type: PNH 38877 Kondo & Edaño (L, holo; A, BM, iso), Sulu Archipelago: Mt Caducan, Jan/Feb-1957. = Cycas circinalis ssp. riumininana var. curranii forma maritima J.Schust. , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (1) (1932) 69; Type: BS 15660 Fenix (K, lecto; L, US, isolecto), Cagayan Island Group, Sulu Sea, Calusa Island, 29-Jul-1912. ≠ Cycas rumphii auct. non Miq. ; Amoroso, PJS 115 (1986) 182.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. BANCALAN, BASILAN, CALUSA, CULION, DANJUGAN (photos), DINAGAT (photos), HAMORAON, HOMONHON, LUZON, MINDANAO: Davao Occidental, Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, OLANGO, PALAWAN, PANAY, SIARGAO, ?SIQUIJOR (photos), SULU ARCHIPELAGO.
species.notes: Coastal vegetation and lowland rainforests immediately behind it. Rarely venturing far from the coast. 0(-?)m.

species.citation: Agoo, Madulid & J.R.Callado , Mem. New York Bot. Gard. 117 (2019) 533; Type: Agoo 10-086 (PNH, holo; DLSUH, iso), Mindanao: Bukidnon, Libona, 300m, Mar-2010.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. MINDANAO: Bukidnon.

species.citation: A.Lindstr. & K.D.Hill , Telopea 12 (2008) 136; Type: BS 48953 Ramos & Edaño (NY, holo; BM, BO, P, iso), Mindanao: Davao Oriental, Mati, Mar/Apr-1927.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao Oriental.
species.notes: Forests over ultramafics on serpentine soil.

species.citation: Agoo, Madulid & J.R.Callado , Mem. New York Bot. Gard. 117 (2019) 531; Type: Casumpang 12-103 (PNH, holo; DLSUH, iso), Mindanao: Davao Oriental, Mati, Brgy Macambol, Sitio Wagon, May 2012.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. MINDANAO: Davao Oriental.

species.citation: K.D.Hill & A.Lindstr. , Telopea 12 (2008) 142; Type: Female plant cultivated at Sydney Botanical Gardens (NSW 728768, holo), from Rapu-Rapu Island, E of Albay, Luzon, 2006.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. ALABAT, CALAYAN (photos), CAMIGUIN DE BABUYANES (photos), DALUPIRI, LUZON: Quezon, POLILLO, RAPU-RAPU.

species.citation: M.Porte ex Regel , Gartenflora 12 (1863) 16 ; De Laubenfels & Adema, Blumea 43 (1998) 382; Lindstrom et al., Telopea 12 (2008) 132; Batuyong et al., Biodiversitas 21 (2020) 3973; Villanueva et al., Biodiversitas 23 (2022) 6498; ≡ Cycas circinalis ssp. riuminiana (Porte ex Regel) J.Schust. , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (1) (1932) 68; Type: Regel 62.10 (K, lecto; Because of uncertainties on the status of this specimen as an authentic original material, Lindstrom et al. suggest designating this as neotype), cult. in Manila. = Cycas chamberlainii W.H.Br. & R.Kienholz , PJS 26 (1925) 47 ; Merrill, EPFP 4 (1926) 241; P.M. Zamora & Co, Guide Philipp. Fl. and Fauna 2 (1986) 219; Amoroso, PJS 115 (1986) 181; Type: BS 42539 Brown & Kienholz (PNH, holo, lost; A, BM, K, L, NY 2 sheets, P, US, iso), Luzon: Pampanga, Mt Arayat, 23-May-1923.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BATAN, LEYTE, LUZON: Bataan, Batangas, Cagayan, Cavite, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, Laguna, NCR, Pampanga, Zambales (photos), MINDORO, SAMAR.
species.notes: Understorey of lowland rain forests, forested areas on ridges and mountains, in closed mixed evergreen forests usually on steep slopes, also in disturbed areas, often on limestone. 0-1030m.

species.citation: Agoo & Madulid , Blumea 57 (2012) 131; Type: Agoo 10-085 (PNH, holo; DLSU, iso), Mindanao, Misamis Oriental, Cagayan de Oro, Cugman River Watershed, disturbed lowland forests, Mar-2010.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. ?BOHOL (photos), MINDANAO: Bukidnon (photos), Misamis Oriental.

species.citation: K.D.Hill & A.Lindstr. , Telopea 12 (2008) 128; Type: SMHI 2035 Podzorski (L, holo), Palawan: St Paul’s Bay, major outcrop to SE, pendulous on limestone cliff face, 6-Apr-1984.
species.notes: Known only from St Paul’s mountain massif, in crevices of vertical limestone cliffs with no soil.

species.citation: A.Lindstr. & K.D.Hill , Telopea 12 (2008) 134; Type: Lindstrom 06/008 (LBC, holo; NSW, BKF, iso), Panay: Iloilo, Barotac Viejo, Brgy Lipata, Sitio Nagpana, Mt Opao, 4-Mar-2006.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CEBU, LEYTE, LUZON: Camarines Sur, MARINDUQUE, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY, SAMAR.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 60 (1936) 234 ; P.M. Zamora & Co, Guide Philipp. Fl. and Fauna 2 (1986) 222; Amoroso, PJS 115 (1986) 183; de Laub. & Adema, Blumea 43 (1998) 385; Lindstrom et al., Telopea 12 (2008) 124; Type: W.H.Brown s.n. (PNH, holo, lost; B, G, GH, K, L 2 sheets, MO 4 sheets, NY 4 sheets, iso), P. Culion Is. ≠ Cycas cairnsiana auct. non F.Muell. ; Merr., EPFP 1 (1922) 1.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CULION, LUZON: NCR, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Seasonally dry Imperata grassland periodically subjected to fire.

species.citation: Madulid & Agoo , Blumea 50 (2005) 520 ; Lindstrom et al., Telopea 12 (2008) 138; Type: PNH 173358 Madulid & Agoo (PNH, holo), Luzon: Zambales, San Antonio, Kawag, Bucao. Open grasslands on ultrabasic hills.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Zambales.
species.notes: Only known from the type locality. Seasonally dry hilly grassland over chromite-rich ultramafics, scattered. 300m.

species.citation: Thunb. , Nova Acta Regiae Soc. Sci. Upsal., 2nd Ser. 4 (1783) 40 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 65; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 1; De Laubenfels & Adema, Blumea 43 (1998) 384; Lindstrom et al., Telopea 12 (2008).
